channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Supreme Court Strikes Down Limits on Political Party Spending
item.description: At issue in the case was a post-Watergate law that Congress passed to limit the amount of money individuals can give to political parties.
item.content:encoded:
At issue in the case was a post-Watergate law that Congress passed to limit the amount of money individuals can give to political parties.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Grady Martin

item.title: Tricks to help you spend less money at restaurants
item.description: Personal finance and nutrition experts share simple strategies that make it possible to eat out without spending a fortune. One tip? You might have to let go of your fast food delivery habit.
item.content:encoded: Personal finance and nutrition experts share simple strategies that make it possible to eat out without spending a fortune. One tip? You might have to let go of your fast food delivery habit.
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: Supreme Court upholds bans on transgender athletes participating in women and girls' sports
item.description: Justice Brett Kavanaugh, who has long coached his daughters' and other girls' basketball teams at school, wrote the court's majority opinion.
item.content:encoded:
Justice Brett Kavanaugh, who has long coached his daughters' and other girls' basketball teams at school, wrote the court's majority opinion.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: SCOTUS to rule on birthright citizenship. And, U.S. murder rate nears new low
item.description: The Supreme Court is expected to make a long-awaited ruling on birthright citizenship today, on the high court's last day of its term. And, the U.S. murder rate approaches a record low.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court is expected to make a long-awaited ruling on birthright citizenship today, on the high court's last day of its term. And, the U.S. murder rate approaches a record low.

(Image credit: Tom Brenner)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: After Trump's re-election, these U.S. scientists found jobs in the U.K.
item.description: More U.S. scientists are heading abroad. Three researchers explain why they decided to shift their research to universities in the U.K.
item.content:encoded: More U.S. scientists are heading abroad. Three researchers explain why they decided to shift their research to universities in the U.K.
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: Here are Colorado's 2026 primary election results
item.description: Live election results: Get the latest on Colorado's U.S. Senate, U.S. House and gubernatorial primary races.
item.content:encoded: Live election results: Get the latest on Colorado's U.S. Senate, U.S. House and gubernatorial primary races.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: U.S. murder rate approaches a record low
item.description: A few years ago, experts worried about a "new normal" of elevated violent crime in the U.S. Now the country is flirting with breaking its all-time low murder rate
item.content:encoded:
A few years ago, experts worried about a "new normal" of elevated violent crime in the U.S. Now the country is flirting with breaking its all-time low murder rate

(Image credit: Mark Stockwell)
item.dc:creator: Martin Kaste

item.title: Venezuelans deported from the U.S. were killed hours later in powerful quakes
item.description: On June 24, 146 Venezuelans were deported from Texas to Caracas. Hours later, while the deportees were in a guarded hotel, powerful twin earthquakes struck.
item.content:encoded:
On June 24, 146 Venezuelans were deported from Texas to Caracas. Hours later, while the deportees were in a guarded hotel, powerful twin earthquakes struck.

(Image credit: Matias Delacroix)
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán

item.title: Federal money for workforce training begins, but few programs qualify
item.description: July 1 marks the official opening of a program that allows federal dollars to go toward short-term workforce training programs. So far just 12 states have created road maps for colleges to apply.
item.content:encoded:
July 1 marks the official opening of a program that allows federal dollars to go toward short-term workforce training programs. So far just 12 states have created road maps for colleges to apply.

(Image credit: Annelise Capossela for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny

item.title: Inside the coordinated strategy to radically reshape U.S. immigration
item.description: As the Supreme Court today weighs the Trump administration's effort to revoke birthright citizenship, NPR looks at what else the White House has done to curb illegal and legal migration.
item.content:encoded: As the Supreme Court today weighs the Trump administration's effort to revoke birthright citizenship, NPR looks at what else the White House has done to curb illegal and legal migration.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo
